title: Digital Chamber amicus brief urges dismissal of NY lawsuit over 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
summary: The Digital Chamber filed an amicus brief urging the dismissal of a New York lawsuit seeking to claim 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, arguing it would set a dangerous precedent for self-custodial wallets.
body:
The Digital Chamber, a prominent blockchain advocacy group, has filed an amicus brief urging the dismissal of a New York lawsuit that seeks to claim ownership of 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets. The lawsuit argues that these wallets should be considered abandoned property. The Digital Chamber contends that allowing such a claim would set a dangerous precedent for self-custodial wallets, potentially undermining the principle of individual ownership in cryptocurrency.

The brief emphasizes that self-custodial wallets are a fundamental aspect of Bitcoin's design, enabling users to maintain full control over their assets. The Digital Chamber warns that if the court rules in favor of the plaintiff, it could encourage similar lawsuits targeting inactive wallets, creating uncertainty and risk for Bitcoin users. The case highlights the ongoing tension between traditional property laws and the decentralized nature of cryptocurrencies.

This legal battle has significant implications for everyday Bitcoin users who rely on self-custodial wallets for security and privacy. A ruling against the defendants could discourage individuals from using personal wallets, pushing them toward custodial services that are more vulnerable to hacks and regulatory interventions. The outcome of this case may influence how courts worldwide approach the ownership of dormant cryptocurrency wallets.

For those interested in the future of Bitcoin and self-custody, this case is a critical one to follow. The Digital Chamber's involvement signals the broader industry's concern about the potential ramifications. Users should stay informed about the case's progress, as any ruling could impact the way they manage their Bitcoin holdings. Watch for updates on the court's decision, which could come in the next few months.
twitterThread: A NY lawsuit wants to claim 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets. The Digital Chamber filed an amicus brief urging dismissal, warning it could threaten self-custody for all users.
Self-custodial wallets are key to Bitcoin's security. The Digital Chamber argues that ruling against wallet owners sets a dangerous precedent.

This case could impact how you hold Bitcoin. Stay tuned for updates—it might change how courts view dormant wallets. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-07-digital-chamber-defends-bitcoin-wallets-in-ny-legal-battle
standaloneTweet: The Digital Chamber filed an amicus brief urging dismissal of a NY lawsuit over 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, warning it could set a dangerous precedent for self-custody rights.
